installed_codex_version.description: Output of the local `codex --version`, or `null` if `codex` is not on `PATH` or the check failed.

schema_codex_version.description: The Codex version this crate's vendored protocol schema was generated from (`schema/CODEX_VERSION.txt`).

RestApprovalPolicy.description: Approval policy preset applied while collecting turn events for `POST /v1/text-turn`. `allow_all` requires `RestRouterOptions::with_unsafe_client_options(true)` and is rejected with `403` otherwise.

params.description: JSON-RPC params for the target method. Defaults to `null` when omitted.

method.description: The method that was called (echoes the path parameter).

callTimeoutMs.description: Per-call JSON-RPC timeout override in milliseconds. Defaults to `codex_app_server_client::DEFAULT_CALL_TIMEOUT` (120s) when omitted.

command.description: Override the `codex` executable path/name to spawn. Requires `RestRouterOptions::with_unsafe_client_options(true)`; rejected with `403` otherwise, since it lets the caller choose an arbitrary host executable.

config.description: Extra `-c key=value` app-server config overrides. Requires `RestRouterOptions::with_unsafe_client_options(true)`; rejected with `403` otherwise. Omitted from the wire format when empty.

extraArgs.description: Extra arguments passed to the spawned `codex app-server` process. Requires `RestRouterOptions::with_unsafe_client_options(true)`; rejected with `403` otherwise. Omitted from the wire format when empty.

name.description: Client name reported to the app-server's `initialize` call. Defaults to a per-route value (e.g. `codex_app_server_rest`) when omitted.

version.description: Client version reported to `initialize`. Defaults to this crate's own version when omitted.

code.description: JSON-RPC error code sent back to the app-server.

data.description: Optional JSON-RPC error data. Omitted (defaults to `null`) when not supplied.

message.description: JSON-RPC error message sent back to the app-server.

code.description: JSON-RPC error code, present only for `error: "json_rpc_error"` (a JSON-RPC error propagated from the app-server). Omitted otherwise.

data.description: JSON-RPC error data, present only for `error: "json_rpc_error"` when the app-server supplied one. Omitted otherwise.

RestEventResponse.description: One session event. Internally tagged on `event`: `notification` (a server notification arrived), `request` (a server-originated request arrived and awaits a reply), `closed` (the session's transport closed), or `timeout` (no event arrived within the poll budget - a normal outcome, not an error).

notification.description: The raw app-server JSON-RPC notification (has its own `method`/`params`).

method.description: The server-originated JSON-RPC method name, e.g. `currentTime/read`.

requestId.description: The JSON-RPC request id (typically a number or string) as sent by the app-server.

requestKey.description: Opaque key for replying via the `.../requests/{requestKey}/result` or `/error` routes.

result.description: The JSON-RPC result to send back to the app-server.

sessionId.description: Opaque session identifier for use in subsequent bridge calls.

model.description: Model override for the ephemeral thread. Uses the app-server's own default when omitted.

prompt.description: The text prompt to send. Rejected with `400` if empty or whitespace-only.

agentMessage.description: Concatenated assistant message text observed for the turn. Empty string if none.

errors.description: Turn error events observed while collecting the turn. Empty array if none.

items.description: One turn-level error event, in the app-server's own shape.

latestDiff.description: The most recent unified diff observed for the turn, or `null` if none.

turnStatus.description: The turn's terminal status (e.g. `"completed"`), or `null` if it could not be determined.

client_notifications.description: Number of client->server notification methods in the vendored schema.

client_requests.description: Number of client->server request methods in the vendored schema.

server_notifications.description: Number of server->client notification methods in the vendored schema.

server_requests.description: Number of server->client request methods in the vendored schema.

bearerAuth.description: Opt-in via `rest::bearer_auth(token)`; not required by the base router. See this document's top-level `info.description`.

info.description:
HTTP surface for the optional `rest` feature of `codex-app-server-client` - a portable adapter around local `codex app-server` JSON-RPC processes. This is only an adapter: it does not authenticate callers, authorize requests, sandbox clients, or make the upstream app-server safe to expose on a network by itself.

**Routes are opt-in per router constructor** - see each operation's description for which of `rest::router()` (health/compat only), `rest::text_turn_router()`, or `rest::trusted_bridge_router()` mounts it. `rest::router_with_options`/`_with_backend*` let a host application mix and match via `RestRouterOptions`.

**Authentication is opt-in and not part of the base router.** Wrap any router in `rest::bearer_auth(token)` (a `tower` `Layer`) to require an `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header on every request except (by default) `GET /health` and `GET /v1/health` - see `BearerAuthLayer::allow_unauthenticated_health`. Operations below that can return `401` note that it only applies once this layer is added; the base router never returns `401` on its own. A caller that presents the one configured token gets everything the mounted router exposes - this is transport auth only, not per-session or per-method authorization.
info.title: codex-app-server-client REST adapter

get.description: Always returns `200`. Never requires authentication, even when `rest::bearer_auth` is layered on (see `BearerAuthLayer::allow_unauthenticated_health`, default `true`).

post.description: Starts a fresh, short-lived Codex session, calls the named app-server method once, and returns its raw JSON-RPC result. Does not preserve turn state, stream events, or let you answer server-originated requests afterward - use the stateful bridge (`POST /v1/sessions` + `POST /v1/sessions/{sessionId}/call/{method}`) for that. Only mounted when `RestRouterOptions::enable_bridge_routes` is `true` (`rest::trusted_bridge_router()`, or `RestRouterOptions::trusted_bridge()`) - neither `rest::router()` nor `rest::text_turn_router()` exposes this route. **Never mount this behind a public/untrusted boundary without your own authentication layer** (e.g. `rest::bearer_auth`) - see this document's top-level description.

parameters.description: Full `codex app-server` JSON-RPC method name, e.g. `thread/start` or `config/read`. IMPORTANT: this is captured by an axum `{*method}` wildcard, not a conventional single-segment path parameter - the value legitimately contains literal `/` characters, so naive path-templating clients that escape `/` in path parameters will build the wrong URL. A leading/trailing `/` on the captured value is trimmed server-side before use.
parameters.name: method

requestBody.description: JSON-RPC params for `method`, plus optional client overrides.

400.description: Malformed JSON body, or the `{method}` path segment was empty.

401.description: Missing or invalid `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header. Only returned when the router is wrapped in `rest::bearer_auth(...)` - the base router has no built-in auth and never returns this on its own.

429.description: `RestLimits::max_one_shot_concurrency` concurrent one-shot calls are already in flight.

502.description: The underlying `codex app-server` process failed, disconnected, or the call itself returned a JSON-RPC error.

get.description: Reports the vendored protocol schema's Codex version, the locally installed `codex --version` (if any), and a generated method-count summary. Requires authentication when `rest::bearer_auth` is layered on (unlike `/health`) since it reveals environment details.

401.description: Missing or invalid `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header. Only returned when the router is wrapped in `rest::bearer_auth(...)` - the base router has no built-in auth and never returns this on its own.

500.description: The backend's compatibility check failed (e.g. the check task panicked or was cancelled).

get.description: Always returns `200`. Never requires authentication, even when `rest::bearer_auth` is layered on (see `BearerAuthLayer::allow_unauthenticated_health`, default `true`).

Lists session IDs for currently open stateful bridge sessions. Only mounted when `RestRouterOptions::enable_bridge_routes` is `true` (`rest::trusted_bridge_router()`, or `RestRouterOptions::trusted_bridge()`) - neither `rest::router()` nor `rest::text_turn_router()` exposes this route. **Never mount this behind a public/untrusted boundary without your own authentication layer** (e.g. `rest::bearer_auth`) - see this document's top-level description.

401.description: Missing or invalid `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header. Only returned when the router is wrapped in `rest::bearer_auth(...)` - the base router has no built-in auth and never returns this on its own.

post.description: Spawns a persistent `codex app-server` process and returns a `sessionId` plus its raw `initialize` response. Use the returned `sessionId` with `POST /v1/sessions/{sessionId}/call/{method}` to drive it. Only mounted when `RestRouterOptions::enable_bridge_routes` is `true` (`rest::trusted_bridge_router()`, or `RestRouterOptions::trusted_bridge()`) - neither `rest::router()` nor `rest::text_turn_router()` exposes this route. **Never mount this behind a public/untrusted boundary without your own authentication layer** (e.g. `rest::bearer_auth`) - see this document's top-level description.

401.description: Missing or invalid `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header. Only returned when the router is wrapped in `rest::bearer_auth(...)` - the base router has no built-in auth and never returns this on its own.

429.description: `RestLimits::max_sessions` concurrently open bridge sessions are already open.

502.description: The `codex app-server` process failed to spawn or initialize.

delete.description: Removes the session and terminates its owned `codex app-server` process once no client clones remain. Only mounted when `RestRouterOptions::enable_bridge_routes` is `true` (`rest::trusted_bridge_router()`, or `RestRouterOptions::trusted_bridge()`) - neither `rest::router()` nor `rest::text_turn_router()` exposes this route. **Never mount this behind a public/untrusted boundary without your own authentication layer** (e.g. `rest::bearer_auth`) - see this document's top-level description.

parameters.description: REST bridge session identifier returned as `sessionId` by `POST /v1/sessions` (the built-in `CodexRestBackend` mints values shaped like `session-<uuid-v4-simple>`, but that format is not a contract - callers must treat it as an opaque token).
parameters.name: sessionId

401.description: Missing or invalid `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header. Only returned when the router is wrapped in `rest::bearer_auth(...)` - the base router has no built-in auth and never returns this on its own.

404.description: No session with this `sessionId` is open.

post.description: Calls any app-server method on an already-open session, preserving thread/turn state across calls (e.g. `thread/start` then `turn/start`). Unlike the one-shot `POST /v1/call/{method}`, the request body's `client` field is rejected (`400`) here - client overrides only apply at session creation or on one-shot calls, not per-call on an existing session. Only mounted when `RestRouterOptions::enable_bridge_routes` is `true` (`rest::trusted_bridge_router()`, or `RestRouterOptions::trusted_bridge()`) - neither `rest::router()` nor `rest::text_turn_router()` exposes this route. **Never mount this behind a public/untrusted boundary without your own authentication layer** (e.g. `rest::bearer_auth`) - see this document's top-level description.

parameters.description: REST bridge session identifier returned as `sessionId` by `POST /v1/sessions` (the built-in `CodexRestBackend` mints values shaped like `session-<uuid-v4-simple>`, but that format is not a contract - callers must treat it as an opaque token).
parameters.name: sessionId

parameters.description: Full `codex app-server` JSON-RPC method name, e.g. `thread/start` or `config/read`. IMPORTANT: this is captured by an axum `{*method}` wildcard, not a conventional single-segment path parameter - the value legitimately contains literal `/` characters, so naive path-templating clients that escape `/` in path parameters will build the wrong URL. A leading/trailing `/` on the captured value is trimmed server-side before use.
parameters.name: method

requestBody.description: JSON-RPC params for `method`. `client` must be omitted or null.

400.description: Malformed JSON body, the `{method}` path segment was empty, or a non-null `client` was supplied.

401.description: Missing or invalid `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header. Only returned when the router is wrapped in `rest::bearer_auth(...)` - the base router has no built-in auth and never returns this on its own.

404.description: No session with this `sessionId` is open.

502.description: The underlying `codex app-server` process failed, disconnected, or the call itself returned a JSON-RPC error.

post.summary: Call a method on an existing bridge session

get.description: Long-polls for the next server notification or server-originated request on this session, up to `timeoutMs` (clamped to `RestLimits::max_poll_timeout`). Returns `{"event": "timeout"}` if nothing arrived in time - that is a normal `200`, not an error, and callers are expected to poll again. At most one poll (via this route or its SSE counterpart below) may be active per session at a time. Only mounted when `RestRouterOptions::enable_bridge_routes` is `true` (`rest::trusted_bridge_router()`, or `RestRouterOptions::trusted_bridge()`) - neither `rest::router()` nor `rest::text_turn_router()` exposes this route. **Never mount this behind a public/untrusted boundary without your own authentication layer** (e.g. `rest::bearer_auth`) - see this document's top-level description.

parameters.description: REST bridge session identifier returned as `sessionId` by `POST /v1/sessions` (the built-in `CodexRestBackend` mints values shaped like `session-<uuid-v4-simple>`, but that format is not a contract - callers must treat it as an opaque token).
parameters.name: sessionId

parameters.description: Long-poll budget in milliseconds. Defaults to, and is clamped down to, the server's configured `RestLimits::max_poll_timeout` (default 30000ms, overridable via `CODEX_APP_SERVER_REST_MAX_POLL_TIMEOUT_MS`) - a caller-requested value above that ceiling is silently lowered to it, never rejected.
parameters.name: timeoutMs

401.description: Missing or invalid `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header. Only returned when the router is wrapped in `rest::bearer_auth(...)` - the base router has no built-in auth and never returns this on its own.

404.description: No session with this `sessionId` is open.

409.description: An event poll (long-poll or SSE stream) is already active for this session.

410.description: A server-originated request arrived but its reply deadline had already passed before it could be surfaced; the app-server was sent an error reply automatically.

429.description: This session already holds `RestLimits::max_pending_requests_per_session` un-replied-to server requests.

get.description: Same events as `GET /v1/sessions/{sessionId}/events`, streamed as Server-Sent Events instead of returned one at a time: repeatedly polls the backend and forwards every resolved event (including `timeout`, as an application-level heartbeat) as its own `data:` frame until a `closed` event or a backend error ends the stream. IMPORTANT: because the `200` status and `text/event-stream` content type are committed the moment the stream opens, a backend error that would be `404`/`409`/`410`/`429` on the long-poll route instead ends this stream with a terminal `event: error` frame carrying the same `RestErrorResponse` body (minus the HTTP status, since none can be sent at that point) - the *only* HTTP-level error status this route can return is `409`, and only synchronously before the stream opens, when another poll is already active for this session. At most one poll (via this route or its long-poll counterpart above) may be active per session at a time. Keep-alive comment frames are sent every `RestLimits::sse_keep_alive_interval` while no real event is ready. Only mounted when `RestRouterOptions::enable_bridge_routes` is `true` (`rest::trusted_bridge_router()`, or `RestRouterOptions::trusted_bridge()`) - neither `rest::router()` nor `rest::text_turn_router()` exposes this route. **Never mount this behind a public/untrusted boundary without your own authentication layer** (e.g. `rest::bearer_auth`) - see this document's top-level description.

parameters.description: REST bridge session identifier returned as `sessionId` by `POST /v1/sessions` (the built-in `CodexRestBackend` mints values shaped like `session-<uuid-v4-simple>`, but that format is not a contract - callers must treat it as an opaque token).
parameters.name: sessionId

parameters.description: Long-poll budget in milliseconds. Defaults to, and is clamped down to, the server's configured `RestLimits::max_poll_timeout` (default 30000ms, overridable via `CODEX_APP_SERVER_REST_MAX_POLL_TIMEOUT_MS`) - a caller-requested value above that ceiling is silently lowered to it, never rejected.
parameters.name: timeoutMs

text/event-stream.description: One SSE `data:` frame per event, `event:` set to the payload's own `event` discriminant (`notification` | `request` | `closed` | `timeout`). See `RestEventResponse` - the frame body is that exact JSON shape, not wrapped further.

200.description: An SSE stream of events for this session.

401.description: Missing or invalid `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header. Only returned when the router is wrapped in `rest::bearer_auth(...)` - the base router has no built-in auth and never returns this on its own.

409.description: An event poll (long-poll or SSE stream) is already active for this session. This is the only backend-originated error this route can report as an HTTP status - see the operation description.

get.summary: Server-Sent Events counterpart to the long-poll route

post.description: Answers a pending server-originated request (surfaced via a `"event": "request"` payload from the events routes above) with a JSON-RPC error. Only mounted when `RestRouterOptions::enable_bridge_routes` is `true` (`rest::trusted_bridge_router()`, or `RestRouterOptions::trusted_bridge()`) - neither `rest::router()` nor `rest::text_turn_router()` exposes this route. **Never mount this behind a public/untrusted boundary without your own authentication layer** (e.g. `rest::bearer_auth`) - see this document's top-level description.

parameters.description: REST bridge session identifier returned as `sessionId` by `POST /v1/sessions` (the built-in `CodexRestBackend` mints values shaped like `session-<uuid-v4-simple>`, but that format is not a contract - callers must treat it as an opaque token).
parameters.name: sessionId

parameters.description: Opaque key returned as `requestKey` on a `"event": "request"` payload from `GET .../events` or `GET .../events/stream`. Answers exactly one pending server-originated request and is single-use - a second reply attempt with the same key returns `404`.
parameters.name: requestKey

401.description: Missing or invalid `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header. Only returned when the router is wrapped in `rest::bearer_auth(...)` - the base router has no built-in auth and never returns this on its own.

404.description: No session with this `sessionId` is open, or no pending request matches `requestKey`.

410.description: `requestKey` matched a request that has since expired (past `RestLimits::pending_request_ttl` or the app-server's own reply deadline) or was already answered.

post.summary: Reply to a pending server-originated request with an error

post.description: Answers a pending server-originated request (surfaced via a `"event": "request"` payload from the events routes above) with a successful JSON-RPC result. Only mounted when `RestRouterOptions::enable_bridge_routes` is `true` (`rest::trusted_bridge_router()`, or `RestRouterOptions::trusted_bridge()`) - neither `rest::router()` nor `rest::text_turn_router()` exposes this route. **Never mount this behind a public/untrusted boundary without your own authentication layer** (e.g. `rest::bearer_auth`) - see this document's top-level description.

parameters.description: REST bridge session identifier returned as `sessionId` by `POST /v1/sessions` (the built-in `CodexRestBackend` mints values shaped like `session-<uuid-v4-simple>`, but that format is not a contract - callers must treat it as an opaque token).
parameters.name: sessionId

parameters.description: Opaque key returned as `requestKey` on a `"event": "request"` payload from `GET .../events` or `GET .../events/stream`. Answers exactly one pending server-originated request and is single-use - a second reply attempt with the same key returns `404`.
parameters.name: requestKey

401.description: Missing or invalid `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header. Only returned when the router is wrapped in `rest::bearer_auth(...)` - the base router has no built-in auth and never returns this on its own.

404.description: No session with this `sessionId` is open, or no pending request matches `requestKey`.

410.description: `requestKey` matched a request that has since expired (past `RestLimits::pending_request_ttl` or the app-server's own reply deadline) or was already answered.

post.summary: Reply to a pending server-originated request with a result

post.description: Starts a fresh, ephemeral Codex session, sends one text prompt, waits for turn completion (bounded by `RestLimits::max_text_turn_duration`), and returns the assistant's message plus the latest diff and any turn errors. Does not preserve session state across calls - use the stateful bridge routes (`POST /v1/sessions`, ...) for multi-turn conversations. Only mounted when `RestRouterOptions::enable_text_turn_route` is `true` (`rest::text_turn_router()`, or `RestRouterOptions::text_turn()` / `::trusted_bridge()`) - the bare `rest::router()` does not expose this route.

requestBody.description: The prompt and optional model/approval/client overrides.

200.description: The turn reached a terminal state.

400.description: Malformed JSON body, or `prompt` is empty/whitespace-only.

401.description: Missing or invalid `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header. Only returned when the router is wrapped in `rest::bearer_auth(...)` - the base router has no built-in auth and never returns this on its own.

403.description: `approvalPolicy: "allow_all"` or a `client.command`/`extraArgs`/`config` override was requested without `RestRouterOptions::with_unsafe_client_options(true)`.

413.description: Accumulated turn output exceeded `RestLimits::max_text_turn_output_bytes`; the turn was interrupted.

429.description: `RestLimits::max_one_shot_concurrency` concurrent one-shot calls are already in flight.

502.description: The underlying `codex app-server` process failed, disconnected, or returned a JSON-RPC error while starting the thread or turn.

504.description: The turn did not reach a terminal state within `RestLimits::max_text_turn_duration`; it was interrupted.

servers.description: Default loopback bind address used by the `rest_server` example and the `codex-app-server-rest` binary's `text-turn` mode.
